REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (3061 Branch Street, Sacramento, Ca 95815)
Off Calendar Jun-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges.

Ms. Alavi informed the Court that the City of Sacramento has been trying to do a tenant inspection and that it is scheduled for July 8, 2014. The sale was confirmed at $45,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed May 28, 2014. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $43,000.00 is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (167 Crestmont Drive, Oakland, Ca 94619)
Off Calendar Jun-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges.

The sale was confirmed at $635,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed June 3, 2014. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $365,000.00 is required. The amount of the commissions is subject to review of the agreement. Ms. Alavi informed the Court that there is an encumbrance in the amount of $232,485.00 that was not referred to in the petition and that a 6% commissions is allowed in Alameda County.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Perna for and with Rudi Oplatka. Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges (Special Administrator). Gwendolyn Harre for Susan McGuire (Surviving Spouse).

Ms. Harre presented oral argument to support her objections and asked that bond not be released until the extent of damages is determined. The issue of rents for the Sacramento and the Oakland properties was also raised. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Jones. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Sep-11-2013 Text Ruling

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-25-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Triay for Rudi Oplatka (Executor). Emile Calhoun for and with Susan McGuire (Surviving Spouse).

Ms. Calhoun provided the case history and that Ms. McGuire did not receive notice of the petition for probate. The Court stated that a Demurrer hearing is set on September 26, 2013, that the Petition to Determine Entitlement may be better argue after the Demurrer hearing and that with the last hearing before Judge Busch, Rudi's powers was limited with increase bond. Ms. Calhoun expressed her client's concerns. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to grounds for removal of Rudi as the executor and appointment of a neutral party.

The Court declined to grant the Petition to remove Rudi Oplatka as the Executor, granted the request to suspend his powers and appointed Scott Hodges as the Special Administator with Will Annexed . Bond in the amount of $100,000.00 is required. Letters to be expired until further order of the Court. Ms. Calhoun to prepare the order.

With regard to the Petition to Determine Entitlement to Inherit of Spouse, Mr. Triay estimated trial will take two one-half days but the Special Administrator will have to decide to go forward with such petition. Ms. Calhoun does not know how long the trial will take without discovery. The Court set Discovery Status Date on December 18, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Hearing on Demurrer and Motion for Protective Order on September 26, 2013 at 2:30 p.m. is continued to October 24, 2013 at 2:00 p.m. in Dept. 204.

With regard to the Petition to Determine Lack of Entitlement to Inherit of Former Spouse and Stepchild, Counsel agreed that there is no opposition. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of order).

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)

Petition For Orders: 1. Issuing A Citation For And Suspending Powers And Removal Of Representative, And 2. Appointing A Neutral Professional Fiduciary, [Or In The Alternative Increasing Executor'S Bond And Revoking His Iaea Powers]; And 3. Establishing A Secure And Transparent Procedure For Opening Of Decedent'S Safe
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Continued To Sep-09-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with Susan McGuire. Charles Triay for and with Rudy Oplatka (Executor/Respondent).

The Court stated that notice was not given to the Franchise Tax Board. Mr. Triay stated that notice was also not given to the alternate Executor or the Surety Company. The Court noted that numerous petitions have been filed, that there are lots of issues to be sorted out and stated that it was inclined to continue today's petition. The Court also informed the Parties that this litigation has the potential to become very complex and very expensive, encouraged the Parties to consider mediation and to think about whether there is a Party that is willing to administer the Estate that the Parties can agree on. Ms. Calhoun asked the Court to suspend the Executor's powers until the next hearing, to increase bond and expressed her Clients concerns. Mr. Triay stated that bond was set at $10,000.00 because his Client is out of state, that the will waives bond, that he has no problem increasing bond, and suggested changing the Executor's powers to limited authority and set bond at $50,000.00. The Petition for Orders Issuing a Citation and Suspending Powers, etc. was continued to September 9, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for proper notice. The Court ordered that the Executor's authority is limited authority with no power to enter into transcations with the real property and that bond be increased to $100,000.00 as an interim relief. Ms. Calhoun is to prepare an order, present it to Mr. Triay and submit it to the Court.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton. (Not Reported)
